inline_review:
commit 66c7e705cbd2a6e05161888c9ad04bc2ceac0747
Author: Ke Zhao <ke.zhao.kernel@gmail.com>

mm, KMSAN: Add missing shadow memory initialization in special allocation paths

This commit explicitly calls kmsan_alloc_page() after successful allocations
in certain page allocation paths to prevent stale KMSAN shadow memory.

Reported-by: syzbot+2aee6839a252e612ce34@syzkaller.appspotmail.com
Closes: https://syzkaller.appspot.com/bug?extid=2aee6839a252e612ce34

> diff --git a/mm/page_alloc.c b/mm/page_alloc.c
> --- a/mm/page_alloc.c
> +++ b/mm/page_alloc.c

[ ... ]

> @@ -5189,6 +5189,10 @@ unsigned long alloc_pages_bulk_noprof(gfp_t gfp, int preferred_nid,
>  
>  		prep_new_page(page, 0, gfp, 0);
>  		set_page_refcounted(page);
> +
> +		trace_mm_page_alloc(page, 0, gfp, ac.migratetype);
> +		kmsan_alloc_page(page, 0, gfp);
> +
>  		page_array[nr_populated++] = page;
>  	}

Could this cause a sleeping in atomic context regression or deadlock?

This loop executes while holding the pcp->lock, which is acquired via
pcp_spin_trylock() and disables interrupts and preemption.

The call to kmsan_alloc_page() saves the stack trace via the stack depot,
which may perform memory allocations using the provided gfp flags. If the
caller passed flags that allow blocking, such as GFP_KERNEL, the stack
depot will attempt a sleeping allocation while IRQs are disabled.

Additionally, executing tracepoints inside an IRQ-disabled lock can
introduce severe latency if BPF programs are attached.

Should these post-allocation hooks be deferred until after
pcp_spin_unlock() is called?

[ ... ]

> @@ -7117,6 +7127,9 @@ int alloc_contig_frozen_range_noprof(unsigned long start, unsigned long end,
>  
>  		check_new_pages(head, order);
>  		prep_new_page(head, order, gfp_mask, 0);
> +
> +		trace_mm_page_alloc(page, order, gfp_mask, get_pageblock_migratetype(page));
> +		kmsan_alloc_page(page, order, gfp_mask);
>  	} else {
>  		ret = -EINVAL;
>  		WARN(true, "PFN range: requested [%lu, %lu), allocated [%lu, %lu)\n",

Does this code introduce a compilation error?

In the __GFP_COMP branch of alloc_contig_frozen_range_noprof(), the
allocated page pointer is assigned to the local variable head:
struct page *head = pfn_to_page(start);

The newly added lines use the variable page, which appears to be undeclared
in this scope. Should head be used here instead?
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"Compilation Error: use of undeclared variable `page` in `alloc_contig_frozen_range_noprof()`.","severity":"Low","severity_explanation":"In the `__GFP_COMP` branch of `alloc_contig_frozen_range_noprof()`, the allocated page pointer is assigned to the local variable `struct page *head = pfn_to_page(start);`. However, the newly added lines call `trace_mm_page_alloc(page, ...)` and `kmsan_alloc_page(page, ...)`. Since `page` is undeclared in this scope, this directly results in a compilation failure (`error: ‘page’ undeclared`). As per the guidelines, build issues are classified as Low severity."},{"problem":"Sleeping in atomic context and deadlock risk due to calling `kmsan_alloc_page()` while holding the PCP spinlock in `alloc_pages_bulk_noprof()`.","severity":"High","severity_explanation":"The patch adds `kmsan_alloc_page(page, 0, gfp)` and `trace_mm_page_alloc()` inside the batch allocation loop in `alloc_pages_bulk_noprof()`. This loop executes while holding `pcp->lock` (acquired via `pcp_spin_trylock()`), which disables interrupts/preemption. `kmsan_alloc_page()` saves the stack trace of the allocation via the stack depot, which may perform memory allocations using the provided `gfp` flags. If the caller passed flags that allow blocking (e.g., `GFP_KERNEL` containing `__GFP_DIRECT_RECLAIM`), the stack depot will attempt a sleeping allocation while IRQs are disabled, immediately triggering a 'sleeping in atomic context' BUG or causing a deadlock. (Note that the existing code explicitly skips bulk allocation if `CONFIG_PAGE_OWNER` is enabled for this exact reason). Additionally, executing tracepoints inside an IRQ-disabled lock can introduce severe latency if BPF programs are attached. These post-allocation hooks must be deferred until after `pcp_spin_unlock()`."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit 66c7e705cbd2a6e05161888c9ad04bc2ceac0747
